title: Compute break-even, net expectancy, segment breakdowns, and random baseline from real trade history

context.problem: 277 closed trades show -$91 total PnL. Pre-audit orientation reveals the top 5 trades account for +$1,111 in a -$91 total — meaning the true underlying PnL ex-outliers is approximately -$1,202. Confidence appears to be INVERTED (higher confidence → lower win rate). No clean-gate era has been proven profitable. The full historical record cannot be trusted as an edge signal without segmentation and outlier analysis.
key_facts_from_orientation: Actual win rate: 26.2% — below gross break-even
fees_usd recorded separately: avg $1.97/trade, total $545 — if pnl_usd is gross, true net PnL is -$636
Top 3 trades (lad-tor ids 183/184/185): +$708 combined from identical duplicate entries — probable bug
Trades 310 and 316: +$216 and +$187 from entries at 0.01/0.011 — ungated anomalies, not repeatable under MIN_ENTRY_PRICE=0.22
BUY_NO: 32.5% win rate, +$5.79 avg. BUY_YES: 23.6% win rate, -$2.90 avg
Confidence inverted: 0.30-0.40 bucket outperforms 0.60-0.65 bucket significantly
context.why_this_matters: Before any strategy change, model fix, or threshold adjustment, we need a single authoritative audit of what the current trade history actually says about expectancy. No code should change based on vibes.

objective: Produce a complete, honest, segment-by-segment analysis of the bot's trade history. Answer every question in the edge proof framework. State pass/fail for each criterion.
required_work: 0. Clarify fee accounting: read paper_exec.py to determine whether pnl_usd includes or excludes fees_usd. Compute both gross and net expectancy.
1. Break-even win rate: compute with and without fees. State the threshold clearly.
2. Overall expectancy: gross and net, all 277 trades.
3. Rolling windows: last 25, 50, 100 trades — wins, losses, win%, total pnl, avg pnl (gross and net).
4. Era breakdown: (a) id≤200 early, (b) id 201-237 pre-gate-wire, (c) id 238-277 post-gate-wire broken runtime, (d) post-clean-restart. Report each era's gross and net expectancy.
5. Outlier removal: (a) remove lad-tor slug (ids 169-187), (b) remove trades 310 and 316, (c) remove all trades with entry_px < 0.22. Report expectancy after each removal.
6. Confidence bucket analysis: for n≥10 per bucket, report win rate and avg pnl. State whether confidence is monotonically predictive.

7. Entry price bucket analysis: use gate-relevant boundaries (<0.05, 0.05-0.10, 0.10-0.15, 0.15-0.22, 0.22-0.30, 0.30-0.40, 0.40-0.50, 0.50-0.70, ≥0.70). State which buckets the current MIN_ENTRY_PRICE=0.22 gates out.
8. Side breakdown: BUY_YES vs BUY_NO — wins, win%, total pnl, avg pnl. State whether the asymmetry is statistically robust at current sample sizes.
9. Close reason breakdown: take_profit, stop_loss, gap_stop, near_resolution, trailing_stop — n, win%, total pnl, avg pnl.
10. Per-slug analysis: for slugs with n≥5, report wins, win%, total pnl. Identify which single slug contributes the most to total positive PnL.
11. Random-side baseline: for each trade, assume side was random (50/50). Compute expected PnL if side were flipped on all trades. This is the 'random side' baseline.
12. Gated-universe projection: for trades where confidence≥0.65 AND entry_px≥0.22, report expectancy. If n<10, state the sample is too small to evaluate.
13. Single-trade dominance: what % of total positive PnL comes from the single best trade? Single best slug?
14. State pass/fail for E1-E8 as defined in EDGE_PROOF_FRAMEWORK_001.md.
acceptance_criteria: Fee accounting clarified: is pnl_usd gross or net?
Break-even win rate stated with and without fees
Rolling window stats reported for last 25/50/100
Confidence bucket table with win rates — monotonicity stated
Side asymmetry reported and rough significance assessed
verification_required: All numbers derived from direct SQLite queries — show the key queries used
Fee accounting method confirmed by reading paper_exec.py
E1-E8 verdict stated with evidence for each
